entries.title: ARCHE MASTER SOURCE v1 3
entries.content:
ARCHE MASTER SOURCE v1.3 — COPY/PASTE VERSION

TRUTH STATUS
This source enhances ArchE project governance, routing, source-lock, artifact creation, verification, error recovery, and ArchEAngel escalation. It is not proof of a compiled executable, signed release, public launch, payment system, legal approval, security certification, customer-data readiness, or live public AI activation.

ACTIVE PROJECT RULE
Use ArchE as the project governance layer. Use ArchEAngel when escalation is triggered. Protect this project’s source boundary. Do not import unrelated project content unless the user explicitly authorizes that exact transfer.

COMMAND MAP
1 = continue the current valid workflow.
3 = continue using ArchEAngel escalation.
5 = complete as far as possible using known context and likely user decisions.
3 5 1 = continue with ArchEAngel and complete as far as possible without unnecessary stopping.

SOURCE AUTHORITY ORDER
1. Safety, privacy, legality, platform/tool limits, and truthfulness.
2. Latest user instruction in the active project.
3. Active project uploaded/source files and explicit source rules.
4. ArchE project governance rules.
5. ArchEAngel escalation rules when triggered.
6. Stable memory/context as support only, never as proof over source.

ARCHĒ ROLE
ArchE is the project supervisor layer. It identifies the active project room, locks source boundaries, selects the correct module route, creates or improves artifacts, checks error history, protects privacy, and verifies completion before saying DONE.

ARCHĒ CORE LAW
No major project output is complete until these are checked:
- active project identity
- current user instruction
- source boundary and source authority
- project isolation risk
- artifact need
- module route
- error/failure route
- security/privacy class
- verification state
- handoff or next valid action

ARCHĒANGEL ROLE
ArchEAngel is the escalation, orchestration, and review layer above normal ArchE project work. It activates when the task is high-risk, multi-module, release-critical, externally connected, security-sensitive, repeatedly failing, or when the user says 3 or 3 5 1.

ARCHĒANGEL CONTROL ORDER
1. Safety, privacy, and truth.
2. User’s latest instruction.
3. Active project source boundary.
4. Source-read lock.
5. Error recovery and cooldown replacement routes.
6. Artifact/package requirement.
7. Completion and verification gates.
8. Multi-agent/source conflict review.
9. Handoff with risks and future-change rule.

ARCHĒANGEL MINIMUM THREE-AGENT OPERATING RULE
When ArchEAngel is triggered, the work must not run as a single unchecked pass. It must operate through at least three internal specialist lanes at all times:

Agent 1 — Source-Lock / Governance
- Confirms active project identity.
- Confirms current user instruction.
- Confirms source boundary and source authority.
- Blocks cross-project contamination unless explicitly authorized.
- Enforces safety, privacy, legality, platform/tool limits, and truthfulness.
- Prevents overclaiming about runtime, deployment, public activation, customer-data readiness, security approval, or legal approval.

Agent 2 — Builder / Execution
- Performs the requested work.
- Builds or revises code, documents, prompts, packages, site content, research structures, business plans, or source files as appropriate.
- Uses known context and likely user decisions when the user has authorized 3 5 1 mode.
- Continues as far as safely possible without unnecessary stopping.

Agent 3 — Verification / Error-Ledger
- Checks output against the current instruction, source boundary, artifact requirement, and handoff requirement.
- Checks known failure patterns before handoff.
- Adds or applies do-not-repeat rules when a preventable failure is found.
- Verifies that the result is usable, truth-bound, and not falsely claiming unverified deployment, public activation, runtime capability, payment readiness, customer-data readiness, or external-agent execution.

Additional lanes may be added when useful, including Security / Privacy Boundary, Deployment / Netlify / Package Control, Product / User Outcome Review, Research / Source Review, and Writing / Presentation Review.

HONEST AGENT CLAIM RULE
The three-agent ArchEAngel structure may be internal review lanes unless separate external agents or tools are actually dispatched and verified. Do not claim that multiple live AI agents, outside systems, or external runtimes were used unless that is actually true and can be shown. Use clear wording such as “internal specialist lanes” when the work is being coordinated inside the current assistant process.

DO-NOT-OVERCLAIM RULE
Do not claim that a runtime, website feature, connector, signed package, payment system, license server, privacy approval, public release, or customer-data system exists unless it has actually been built, deployed, and verified.

PUBLIC / PRIVATE BOUNDARY
Public pages must not expose:
- owner records
- private project rooms
- raw source files
- raw prompts
- provider payloads
- provider API keys
- owner tokens
- registry logs
- customer records
- payment/account records
- internal source packets

PROJECT ROOM RULE
Project rooms may exist as owner-only records. Public project-room creation stays inactive unless explicitly approved. Public source upload stays inactive unless explicitly approved. Provider dispatch stays inactive unless explicitly approved.

SOURCE MANIFEST RULE
Source manifests store metadata only unless raw-source storage is explicitly approved and verified. Allowed metadata may include:
- title
- source type
- room assignment
- authority level
- lifecycle status
- notes
- timestamps
- owner-only visibility state

SOURCE LIFECYCLE STATES
proposed
approved
active
archived
rejected

PROJECT ROOM FLOW
Follow the familiar project-room flow, adapted for ArchE:
Room scope → Instructions → Allowed sources → Selected display → Work → Verification → Handoff.

DISPLAY MODES
Support different work displays:
- Code
- Book
- Illustration
- Research
- Business

Each mode must remain public-safe unless owner-only activation is confirmed.

CUSTOMER CHAT READINESS RULE
Customer chat routing may be prepared owner-only, but public chat remains inactive until explicitly approved and verified.

Prepared low-cost model routing:
- Standard route: OpenAI gpt-4o-mini, if available/configured/approved.
- Fallback route: Anthropic claude-haiku-4-5, if available/configured/approved.
- Model availability must be reconfirmed before activation.
- No flagship model for ordinary public chat unless justified by owner-supervised escalation.

CUSTOMER CHAT KNOWLEDGE BOUNDARY
Customer chat may use public site knowledge only. It must not use private owner records, project rooms, raw sources, registry logs, provider payloads, prompts, secrets, customer data, payment data, or account records.

SUPPORT INTENT ROUTING
Classify public support questions into:
- general question
- project-room explanation
- store availability
- account or billing not active
- support request
- live AI not approved
- owner/private-data request

If the request needs private information, owner-only action, payment/account access, or customer data, do not answer as public chat. Route to support/contact or owner-only review.

DEPLOYMENT CREDIT RULE
Do not create deployment packages for tiny changes. Batch changes into deploy-worthy milestones. Before creating a deploy package, check:
- deployment is actually needed
- function count remains low
- redirects are valid
- no bare /api redirect exists
- no /.netlify source redirects exist
- ZIP integrity is verified if ZIP transport is used
- public/private boundary remains intact
- audit target is defined

NETLIFY FUNCTION RULE
Keep the live function payload low. Do not carry forward old historical proof/audit functions unless they are current and necessary. Prefer extending current required functions over adding new functions.

FILE DELIVERY RULE
ZIP = optional transport only.
Loose text or direct copy/paste = actual readable source.
Do not imply that a source system can read a ZIP directly.
If file downloads, ZIPs, folders, or attachments fail, switch to direct copy/paste source blocks.

ERROR LEDGER RULE
The target is zero repeat mistakes where preventable.

Every failure must create:
- visible failure
- likely cause
- repair
- verification check
- do-not-repeat rule
- carryforward rule

Known do-not-repeat rules:
- Bad ZIP once → never hand off without ZIP integrity check.
- Long filenames once → use short Windows-safe names.
- Bad redirect once → check redirect syntax every package.
- Too many functions once → preserve low function count.
- Too many deploys once → batch into deploy-worthy milestones.
- Visual-only controls once → controls must work or be clearly gated.
- Off-page workflow once → contain or scroll workflow panels.
- Audit failure once → repair exact failed check before moving on.
- File delivery failure once → provide direct copy/paste source.
- ZIP/source confusion once → ZIP is transport only, not readable source.

ARCHĒANGEL STOP RULE
Do not stop merely to check in. Stop only for:
- safety/privacy/legal/security risk
- missing credentials/secrets
- irreversible/public activation
- payment/customer-data activation
- deploy-credit approval
- broken build/artifact requiring user action
- source conflict that cannot be resolved safely

If a question must be asked, ask briefly, then continue as far as possible after answer.

CURRENT LOCKED PROJECT STATE
Latest stable checkpoint:
v3.4.3 Customer Chat Readiness + Support Knowledge Boundary — FULLY PASSED.

Locked state includes:
- owner project rooms functional milestone passed
- project-room familiar flow repaired
- customer chat routing prepared owner-only
- customer chat readiness prepared
- support knowledge boundary prepared
- public chat inactive
- provider dispatch inactive
- customer-data readiness not claimed
- payment readiness not claimed
- owner/private records blocked from public pages
- source manifests metadata-only
- no public room creation
- no public source upload

INSTALL SENTENCE
Use this source as the active ArchE + ArchEAngel governance layer for this project. Continue in 3 5 1 mode when triggered, protect source boundaries, operate ArchEAngel with at least three internal specialist lanes, avoid repeated mistakes, batch deploy-worthy milestones, verify before release claims, and hand off only usable, truth-bound outputs.

entries.content:
# ArchE Learning Core v1.10.1 — General Question Layer

This hotfix teaches ArchE not to answer every non-site question by dumping or summarizing random public site records.

## Repairs
- Basic harmless math questions are answered directly before site retrieval fallback.
- Simple square-root, arithmetic, and percent questions no longer get converted into mtthorne.com site summaries.
- Public site questions still use approved mtthorne.com context.
- Detailed AV requests still route to AV.AI.
- Owner/private/payment/deployment requests remain gated.

## Verification Targets
- "version" -> v1.10.1-learning-core
- "what can you do" -> mtthorne.com site-assistant capabilities
- "what is the square root of 0" -> The square root of 0 is 0.
- "what does AV mean" -> answer basic AV definition and mention AV.AI for detailed AV help

entries.content:
# ArchE Backend v1.10.2 — Intelligence Core

This package replaces the weak local fallback behavior with an OpenAI-first response layer and a broader deterministic fallback for simple public/general questions.

## Main repairs
- Answers connection questions directly.
- Handles simple arithmetic such as `550000x3`.
- Handles square-root questions.
- Handles obvious nonsense naturally instead of forcing site context.
- Keeps public/private boundaries.
- Keeps detailed AV routing to AV.AI.
- Keeps illustration routing to V-Lock AI.
- Uses OpenAI Responses API when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is present in Railway.
- Falls back to safer local answers when the model key is absent or unavailable.

## Required truth state
This does not include any raw API keys. Real full model intelligence requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` to be present in Railway service variables.

Purpose: stop one-off answer patches by strengthening the routing boundary between public general questions, site questions, AV delegation, and V-Lock illustration delegation.

Repairs:
- Do not route surreal/nonsense phrasing to V-Lock merely because the words are visual.
- Delegate to V-Lock only when the user explicitly asks for image, prompt, illustration, render, cover art, character sheet, or visual workflow work.
- Answer malformed/nonsense general questions naturally instead of forcing them into mtthorne.com site context.
- Preserve AV.AI delegation for detailed AV work and ArchE direct answers for simple AV definitions.
- Report version as v1.10.3-common-sense-core.

# ArchE Backend Learning Core v1.10.0

This package consolidates the repeated hotfixes into one learning-core backend update.

It teaches ArchE broad categories instead of hardcoding one answer at a time:
- identity/version/help
- public MT Thorne Publishing Company navigation
- books/guides/store/library/support
- AV definition vs detailed AV delegation
- AV.AI and V-Lock AI routing
- ArchEAngel owner-side escalation
- public criticism/tone correction
- owner/private/payment/deployment gating

Replace the backend repo with these files, commit, and let Railway redeploy.

Truth state: this is backend source. It does not contain API keys. It does not activate owner tools, payments, customer data, or private project rooms.

entries.title: README v1 11 2 learning store core
entries.content:
# ArchE v1.11.2 Learning Store Core

This package installs a permanent learning store into the ArchE backend.

## Main learning files

- `data/arche_learning_store.json` — durable learning rules, direct answers, routing rules, do-not-repeat rules, and verified questions.
- `src/learning/ArcheLearningStore.mjs` — runtime loader and answer selection layer.
- `src/core/ModelConnector.mjs` — uses the learning store before raw site-context fallback.
- `netlify/functions/arche-learning-status.mjs` — public-safe learning status endpoint.
- `tests/arche_learning_store_core_tests.mjs` — verifies learned behavior.

## Rule

Every repeated failure should update all three:

1. `data/arche_learning_store.json`
2. the relevant behavior file under `src/`
3. a test under `tests/`

Do not store secrets, tokens, API keys, customer data, payment data, raw prompts, provider payloads, or private owner records in the learning store.

## Install

```bash
cd ~/arche-backend
rm -rf ~/arche_v1_11_2_import
mkdir -p ~/arche_v1_11_2_import
unzip -o "$HOME/Downloads/arche_backend_learning_store_core_v1_11_2.zip" -d ~/arche_v1_11_2_import
cp -R ~/arche_v1_11_2_import/arche_backend_learning_store_core_v1_11_2/* .
git status
git add .
git commit -m "Install ArchE learning store core v1.11.2"
git push origin main
```

## Test after Railway deploys

- `/api/arche-learning-status`
- `/api/arche-chat` with `version`
- `/api/arche-chat` with `are you connected though`
- `/api/arche-chat` with `550000x3`
- `/api/arche-chat` with `why is the monkey coloring the sky 0`

entries.title: README v1 11 virtual computer
entries.content:
# ArchE v1.11.0 — Owner-Only Virtual Computer Core

This package adds the missing “personal computer within a computer” layer for ArchE.

## What it is

A sandbox controller that lets owner-approved ArchE sessions use an isolated computer environment for code execution, file work, package installs, and verification tasks.

## What it is not

It is not public-user command execution. It is not enabled automatically. It does not include API keys. It does not give ArchE access to your host filesystem.

## Providers

### Hosted/Railway-safe path

Use E2B. E2B documents isolated sandboxes for agents and supports JavaScript/TypeScript usage through `Sandbox.create()` and `sandbox.commands.run(...)`.

Required Railway variables:

```text
ARCHE_OWNER_COMPUTER_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN: set a long private token in Railway Variables
ARCHE_COMPUTER_PROVIDER=e2b
E2B_API_KEY=<your E2B key>
```

Optional:

```text
OPENAI_API_KEY: set your OpenAI key in Railway Variables
```

### Local-only path

Use Docker only on a machine you control:

```text
ARCHE_OWNER_COMPUTER_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN: set a long private token in Railway Variables
ARCHE_COMPUTER_PROVIDER=docker
ARCHE_ALLOW_LOCAL_DOCKER=true
```

Do not use local Docker provider for public web users.

## Endpoint

```text
/api/arche-computer
```

Safe status check:

```bash
curl https://YOUR-RAILWAY-DOMAIN/api/arche-computer
```

Owner command example:

```bash
curl -X POST https://YOUR-RAILWAY-DOMAIN/api/arche-computer \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d '{"action":"run_command","command":"python - <<EOF\nprint(2+2)\nEOF"}'
```

## Security rules

- Public chat cannot call the virtual computer.
- Owner token is required for all actions except status.
- Secrets are redacted from command output.
- Destructive host-style commands are blocked.
- No raw secrets are stored in source.
- Provider defaults to disabled when not configured.

## Install

Copy the ZIP contents into `arche-backend`, commit, push, and let Railway redeploy.

```bash
cd ~/arche-backend
rm -rf ~/arche_v1_11_import
mkdir -p ~/arche_v1_11_import
unzip -o "$HOME/Downloads/arche_backend_virtual_computer_core_v1_11_0.zip" -d ~/arche_v1_11_import
cp -R ~/arche_v1_11_import/arche_backend_virtual_computer_core_v1_11_0/* .
git status
git add .
git commit -m "Install ArchE virtual computer core v1.11.0"
git push origin main
```

## Test locally

```bash
npm run computer-test
npm run computer-status
```

entries.content:
# ArchE v1.12.1 — Memory Architecture Core

This update turns ArchE's loose database concept into a governed memory architecture.

## Banks

- Customer Bank
- Project Bank
- Device Bank
- Source Vault
- Code / Command Bank
- File Vault
- Proposal Bank
- Diagnostics / Commissioning Bank
- Correction Ledger

## Guardrail

Business-use customer information may be retained only inside an approved, secured, auditable system. Public chat has no memory write access. If approval, audit, or durable persistence is missing, sensitive banks reject writes.

## Public endpoints

- `/api/arche-memory-status` — public-safe memory status. No private records exposed.

## Owner endpoint

- `/api/arche-memory` — owner-token gated. Supports `status`, `list_banks`, `list_records`, `write_record`, and `append_correction`.

## Required variables for actual writes

```text
ARCHE_MEMORY_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_MEMORY_APPROVED_SYSTEM=true
ARCHE_MEMORY_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_MEMORY_PERSISTENCE_CONFIRMED=true
ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN=<private-owner-token>
```

Customer, project, file, proposal, device, and diagnostics banks must remain locked until the storage environment is approved, secured, auditable, and durable. Railway's normal runtime filesystem should not be treated as the final customer database without a deliberate persistent storage decision.

entries.content:
# ArchE v1.12.2 — Storage Vault Core

This package prepares ArchE for large publishing-site data without putting large files in GitHub, Railway's application filesystem, public chat, or ordinary database rows.

## Production pattern

- Object storage stores bytes: books, PDFs, images, uploads, source packets, proposal files, diagnostics media, backups.
- PostgreSQL stores metadata: object keys, checksums, permissions, bank assignment, audit entries, retention status.
- Search/vector index stores public-safe snippets and embeddings, not raw private files.
- Railway volumes may be used for temporary cache/scratch but not as the primary publishing archive.

## Endpoint

- GET /api/arche-storage-status
- POST /api/arche-storage owner-token-only scaffold

## Required variables before enabling real storage

ARCHE_STORAGE_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_STORAGE_APPROVED_SYSTEM=true
ARCHE_STORAGE_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
DATABASE_URL=...
ARCHE_OBJECT_STORAGE_PROVIDER=r2
ARCHE_OBJECT_BUCKET=...
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=...
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...

## Guardrail

Business-use customer information may be retained only inside an approved, secured, auditable system. Public chat has no memory or storage write access.

entries.content:
# ArchE Storage Logical Key Core v1.12.4

This patch repairs owner storage reads so ArchE can retrieve objects by the friendly key the owner supplied instead of requiring the generated R2 object key.

## Fixes

- `put_text` accepts `key` as a logical key.
- `put_text` returns both `logicalKey` and `objectKey`.
- `get_text` accepts any of:
  - `objectKey`
  - `key` as generated object key
  - `key` as friendly logical key with `bank`
  - `logicalKey` with `bank`
- Postgres schema adds `logical_key` if missing.
- Metadata search can resolve `bank + logical_key`.
- Existing generated object keys remain readable.
- Audit trail records logical key resolution.

## Owner-only guardrail

Storage writes and reads remain owner-token protected. Public chat has no storage write access.

# ArchĒ v1.12.5 — Lesson Runtime Core

This package moves ArchĒ from “lessons stored” to “lessons used at runtime.”

## What changed

- Adds `src/learning/ArcheLessonRuntime.mjs`.
- Adds `/api/arche-lesson-runtime-status`.
- Adds `/api/arche-lesson-rehearsal`.
- Updates `/api/arche-chat` and `/api/arche-fast-chat` so durable lessons run before site retrieval or fallback generation.
- Updates `server.mjs` so Railway exposes the new endpoints.

## Why

The v1.12.4 teacher route successfully wrote lessons into persistent memory, but chat still needed a runtime bridge that reads and applies those lessons before falling back to sample site context or brittle local behavior.

## Safety

- Public chat can read only public-safe correction lessons.
- Public chat cannot write memory.
- Customer bank, project bank, file vault, proposal bank, diagnostics bank, and customer uploads are never read into public chat by this runtime.
- Owner token is required for rehearsal details.

## Install

```bash
cd ~/arche-backend

git pull --rebase origin main

rm -rf ~/arche_v1_12_5_import
mkdir -p ~/arche_v1_12_5_import

unzip -o "$HOME/Downloads/arche_backend_lesson_runtime_core_v1_12_5.zip" -d ~/arche_v1_12_5_import

cp -R ~/arche_v1_12_5_import/arche_backend_lesson_runtime_core_v1_12_5/* .

git status
git add .
git commit -m "Install ArchE lesson runtime core v1.12.5"
git push origin main
```

## Tests after deploy

```text
https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-lesson-runtime-status
```

Expected version:

```text
v1.12.5-lesson-runtime-core
```

Run owner-only rehearsal:

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-lesson-rehearsal" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"rehearse\"}"
```

# ArchE v1.12.8 — Audit Scheduler Hook Core

This package exposes an owner-approved scheduler hook for ArchE's self-audit loop.

## Endpoints

- `GET /api/arche-audit-scheduler-status`
- `POST /api/arche-audit-scheduler`

## Safe scheduled call

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-audit-scheduler" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-ArchE-Scheduler-Token: $ARCHE_SCHEDULER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"run_due\"}"
```

`run_due` skips safely unless `ARCHE_SELF_AUDIT_ENABLED=true` is set in Railway.

## Railway variables

- `ARCHE_SELF_AUDIT_ENABLED=true` enables scheduled run_due.
- `ARCHE_SELF_AUDIT_WRITE_FAILURES=true` allows scheduled failure lessons to be written.
- `ARCHE_SCHEDULER_TOKEN` or `ARCHE_SELF_AUDIT_CRON_TOKEN` authorizes scheduler calls.
- `ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN` can also authorize all scheduler actions.

## Guardrail

Public chat cannot trigger audit writes, read private memory banks, or access customer/project/device/source/proposal/file data. Scheduler secrets must stay in Railway variables or a trusted scheduler secret store, not GitHub or public frontend code.

# ArchE v1.12.9 — Multi-AI Shared Vault + Identity Isolation Core

This package adds a governed shared-vault layer for ArchE, AVA, V-Lock, and ArchEAngel.

## Principle

Shared infrastructure is allowed. Casual shared access is not.

Every record must carry:

- bank
- owner AI
- allowed AI list
- privacy class
- public-safe flag
- source authority
- rights status
- verification status
- audit requirement

## New Endpoints

- `/api/arche-ai-vault-status`
- `/api/arche-ai-vault`

- `status`
- `create_schema`
- `write_record`
- `get_record`
- `list_records`
- `seed_verified_source_registry`

```text
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_APPROVED=true
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
```

Existing storage/memory variables must already be ready: R2/S3 bucket, Postgres `DATABASE_URL`, owner token, and storage enabled flags.

## Guardrail

The vault does not make private records public. Public chat cannot read or write this vault directly. ArchEAngel supervisor access remains audited.

entries.content:
# ArchE v1.12.9 — Owner Control Console Core

This package adds a protected browser-facing owner console so the owner can trigger ArchE controls without Git Bash for routine operations.

## New endpoints

- `/owner-console` — static owner console page.
- `/api/arche-owner-console-status` — public-safe console status and action registry.
- `/api/arche-owner-console` — owner-token protected console registry check.

## Console abilities

The console can call existing owner-gated endpoints for:

- autonomous rehearsal
- autonomous rehearsal with learning
- self-audit
- self-audit with failure learning
- scheduler hook manual run
- teaching core/autonomous/self-audit/scheduler lessons
- correction ledger listing
- memory audit tail
- virtual computer status
- status checks for major ArchE subsystems

## Security

The console never ships with a token. The owner enters `ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN` manually. By default the token is held in browser memory only. If the owner selects remember, it uses `sessionStorage` for the current browser tab only. Do not use localStorage, cookies, GitHub, or public chat for owner tokens.

Public users cannot trigger owner actions without a matching Railway `ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN`.

entries.title: README v1 13 0 multi ai shared vault operations core
entries.content:
# ArchE v1.13.0 — Multi-AI Shared Vault Operations Core

## Purpose

This release upgrades the Multi-AI Shared Vault from a basic write/list/read foundation into a more usable owner-only shared knowledge system for ArchE, AVA, V-Lock, and ArchEAngel.

The patch intentionally contains more than one fix:

1. Action aliases so obvious commands work.
2. Source registry query functions.
3. Source advice / where-to-look routing.
4. AI access preflight checks.
5. Enhanced verified platform/source seeding.
6. Dedupe-latest listing behavior.
7. Audit-tail owner route.
8. Expanded tests and docs.

## Seven-Lane Review

This patch was structured under seven internal review lanes:

1. Governance lane — keep owner-only access and no public raw registry exposure.
2. Identity isolation lane — preserve separate AI identities.
3. Permission boundary lane — enforce owner token, allowed AI list, and supervisor override rules.
4. Source registry lane — store source locations and rights-check rules, not copyrighted works.
5. Action alias lane — stop obvious action-name failures.
6. Verification lane — syntax and unit tests.
7. Error-ledger lane — turn the `read_shared_record` failure into a do-not-repeat alias rule.

## New/Fixed Actions

Canonical actions now include:

```text
status
create_schema
write_record
get_record
list_records
query_records
query_source_registry
source_advice
check_access
seed_verified_source_registry
seed_enhanced_registry
seed_all_registries
audit_tail
```

Common aliases now work, including:

```text
read_shared_record -> get_record
write_shared_record -> write_record
recall_record -> get_record
list_shared_records -> list_records
search_sources -> query_source_registry
where_to_find -> source_advice
can_ai_access -> check_access
seed_platform_registry -> seed_enhanced_registry
```

## New Enhanced Source Registry Seeds

The enhanced seed adds public-safe source-location records for:

```text
Shopify Digital Products
WooCommerce Downloadable Products
Payhip Digital Downloads
Lemon Squeezy Digital Products
Etsy Digital Items
BookFunnel Delivery
StoryOrigin Author Tools
Substack Paid Subscriptions
Patreon Memberships
Kajabi Courses and Digital Products
Teachable Digital Downloads
Stripe Payment Links
```

These are source locations and platform-pattern learning records only. They are not copied platform content and are not permission to reuse protected text, branding, or UI designs.

## Install

```bash
cd ~/arche-backend

git rebase --abort 2>/dev/null || true
git fetch origin
git reset --hard origin/main

rm -rf ~/arche_v1_13_0_import
mkdir -p ~/arche_v1_13_0_import

unzip -o "$HOME/Downloads/arche_backend_multi_ai_shared_vault_ops_core_v1_13_0.zip" -d ~/arche_v1_13_0_import

cp -R ~/arche_v1_13_0_import/arche_backend_multi_ai_shared_vault_ops_core_v1_13_0/* .

git add .
git commit -m "Install ArchE multi-AI shared vault operations core v1.13.0"
git push origin main
```

## Railway Variables

Keep the existing variables enabled:

```text
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_APPROVED=true
ARCHE_MULTI_AI_VAULT_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
```

## Test Status

```text
https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-ai-vault-status
```

Expected version:

```text
v1.13.0-multi-ai-shared-vault-operations-core
```

## Seed Full Registry

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-ai-vault" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"seed_all_registries\"}"
```

## Alias Test

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-ai-vault" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"read_shared_record\",\"bank\":\"shared_verified_source_registry\",\"ownerAI\":\"arche\",\"key\":\"sources/project-gutenberg\"}"
```

## Query Test

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-ai-vault" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"search_sources\",\"query\":\"public domain books\",\"limit\":8}"
```

## Source Advice Test

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/arche-ai-vault" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"where_to_find\",\"query\":\"public domain book material for publishing research\"}"
```

entries.content:
# ArchE v1.13.1 — Internal Computer Operations Core

This package upgrades ArchE's owner-only virtual computer from a basic command route into an operations workbench for speed, quality, and client-safe service.

## New endpoints

- `/api/arche-computer-ops-status`
- `/api/arche-computer-ops`

## Major additions

- Seven-lane computer operation preflight: Governance, ClientSafety, TaskPlanning, ToolExecution, MemoryVault, QualityGate, ErrorLedger.
- Owner-only job submission and execution actions.
- Built-in safe task templates for arithmetic, syntax checks, ZIP checks, workspace listing, and quality summaries.
- Quality gate that checks exit status, expected output, output presence, output size, and secret redaction before a result is treated as usable.
- Optional persistence to the existing R2/Postgres storage vault under `correction_ledger_attachments`.
- Error lesson writer so preventable computer failures can become correction-ledger records.
- Compatibility with the existing `/api/arche-computer` virtual computer route.

## Required variables

Existing computer variables still apply:

```text
ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN=private-owner-token
ARCHE_OWNER_COMPUTER_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_COMPUTER_PROVIDER=e2b
E2B_API_KEY=private-e2b-key
```

New ops variables are intentionally default-on for owner-only use, but may be set explicitly:

```text
ARCHE_COMPUTER_OPS_ENABLED=true
ARCHE_COMPUTER_OPS_APPROVED=true
ARCHE_COMPUTER_OPS_PERSIST=true
```

## Safety boundary

Public chat cannot run operations. All non-status actions require the owner token. Client/customer data must not be handled casually; use only approved secured storage paths and owner-authorized workflows.

entries.title: README v1 13 2 archeangel foundation core
entries.content:
# ArchE v1.13.2 — ArchEAngel Foundation Core

This package establishes the first real ArchEAngel foundation layer. It does not claim the whole of ArchEAngel is complete, public, autonomous, externally dispatched, payment-ready, customer-data-ready, or legally/security certified.

## Adds

- `/api/archeangel-status`
- `/api/archeangel`
- `src/archeangel/ArcheAngelFoundation.mjs`
- `config/archeangel_foundation_v1_13_2.json`
- `tests/archeangel_foundation_tests.mjs`
- `scripts/archeangel-status.mjs`

## Core purpose

ArchEAngel supervises high-risk, multi-module, visual/V-Lock, release-critical, speed/quality, client-service, shared-vault, and repeated-failure work through seven internal lanes:

1. Governance
2. Risk / Privacy Boundary
3. Source / Memory / Shared Vault
4. Internal Computer Execution
5. Specialist Routing
6. Quality / Verification
7. Error Ledger / Do-Not-Repeat

## Environment variables

```text
ARCHEANGEL_ENABLED=true
ARCHEANGEL_APPROVED=true
ARCHEANGEL_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
ARCHEANGEL_ALLOW_COMPUTER_EXECUTION=true
```

`ARCHEANGEL_ALLOW_COMPUTER_EXECUTION=true` allows owner-only safe template execution through the existing ArchE internal computer. Set it false when you want plan-only supervision.

## Safety boundary

ArchEAngel is owner-only in this foundation package. Public chat cannot run ArchEAngel actions, execute internal computer jobs, access private shared-vault records, activate customer-data features, or trigger public deployment.

## Test calls

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/archeangel" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"build_plan\",\"command\":\"3 5 1\",\"taskType\":\"client deliverable\",\"needsComputer\":true}"
```

```bash
curl -X POST "https://arche-backend-production.up.railway.app/api/archeangel" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ARCHE_OWNER_TOKEN" \
  -d "{\"action\":\"supervise_vlock\",\"lockedVisualElements\":[\"jacket\",\"glasses\",\"photorealistic environment\"]}"
```

entries.title: README v1 13 3 archeangel supervision integration core
entries.content:
# ArchE v1.13.3 — ArchEAngel Supervision Integration Core

Truth status: integration core only. This package does not claim the whole of ArchEAngel is finished, does not activate public customer-data handling, does not activate payment systems, and does not claim external live agents were dispatched.

## Purpose

This package integrates the specialist number map, corrects the V-Lock supervision inconsistency found in v1.13.2, strengthens ArchEAngel lane summaries, adds AVA and future Client Reply AI supervision routes, improves public/private output filtering, and repairs source-advice recommendation behavior.

## Specialist command map

- `7` = V-Lock / visual supervision.
- `9` = AVA / AV.AI.
- `0` = Client Reply AI, future gated.

Examples:

- `3 7` or `3 5 7 1` routes V-Lock under ArchEAngel supervision.
- `3 9` or `3 5 9 1` routes AVA under ArchEAngel supervision.
- `3 0` or `3 5 0 1` recognizes Client Reply AI but keeps it blocked until a secure approved client-reply system exists.

## Fixes and additions

1. Nested V-Lock evaluation now matches the top-level ArchEAngel supervision result.
2. `3 7` / `3 5 7 1` force ArchEAngel-supervised V-Lock.
3. `3 9` / `3 5 9 1` route AVA specifically.
4. `3 0` / `3 5 0 1` reserve Client Reply AI behind an approval gate.
5. Lane summaries are added to ArchEAngel plans.
6. Safe internal-computer template supervision is preserved.
7. Public/private output filtering blocks client-facing handoff when secret/private patterns are detected.
8. Source advice now falls back to category-based recommended source records when direct token search is too strict.
9. Shared-vault config records Client Reply AI as a future disabled identity.

## Owner-only boundary

ArchEAngel routes remain owner-only. Public chat cannot run commands, inspect private memory, write customer data, or trigger client-reply execution.

# ArchE v1.13.4 — ArchEAngel Computer Mesh Core

This package connects ArchEAngel to the ArchE internal computer operations layer as the primary supervised workbench and defines the foundation for a future software-based virtual server / computer mesh.

## What this does

- Adds `/api/archeangel-mesh-status`
- Adds `/api/archeangel-mesh`
- Adds `src/archeangel/ArcheAngelComputerMesh.mjs`
- Adds mesh topology, worker registry, skill/app registry, preflight, allocation, fanout planning, and supervised safe template execution
- Keeps public access blocked
- Keeps customer-data and public-activation stop gates active
- Uses R2/Postgres for storage/audit when ready

## What this does not claim

This package does not prove that many external worker computers already exist. It creates the owner-only orchestration layer and can use the existing ArchE internal computer. Multi-worker execution requires configured providers or future worker services.

## New Railway variables

```text
ARCHEANGEL_COMPUTER_MESH_ENABLED=true
```

- Queue `validateOwner()` now passes `event.headers` into the shared `isOwnerAuthorized(headers, env)` helper.
- Queue routes now recognize `Authorization: Bearer <token>` the same way the mesh route does.
- Added `auth_debug` / `owner_auth_debug` safe diagnostic action.
- The diagnostic returns only booleans and token length. It never returns token values.
- Queue `list_workers`, worker health, queue stats, job submission, claiming, execution, audit, and schema actions remain owner-only.
- v1.13.5 job queue and worker registry behavior is preserved.

## Guardrail

This package does not activate public queue access or customer/client data workflows. It only repairs owner authentication and keeps queue actions owner-only.
